description: CPU-only INTERACTION (difference-in-differences) power/MDE gate for the CAPSTONE claim (q-0001), refining e-0040/a-0038. e-0040 gave the single-comparison rough-vs-fine pass@1 MDE but only FLAGGED (never quantified) that the headline is a gap x model-SIZE interaction (the gap GROWS as the model shrinks) needing more power. Models four per-problem binary outcomes on the SAME held-out theorems -- {small,large} model x {fine,rough} policy -- via a one-factor Gaussian copula (all six pairwise correlations = rho=0.5, the e-0040 SFT-variant correlation extended to the size axis). Per-problem interaction statistic D = (rough_small-fine_small)-(rough_large-fine_large) in {-2..2}; tests mean(D) with a two-sided z-test, with the large-model gap fixed at 0 (null sub-case) and the small-model fine rate near the e-0005 floor. Finds the smallest small-model gap (= the DiD) reaching 80% power. Pure stdlib; seed 0; M=1500.
motivation: a-0038/e-0040's own load-bearing caveat: 'the headline is a gap x model-SIZE INTERACTION ... a difference-in-differences across sizes is STRICTLY harder to power, so these MDEs are a LOWER bound on what the full interaction claim needs.' That surcharge -- the decision-relevant number for the capstone -- was asserted but never computed. Quantify it cheaply before the Modal go/no-go, reusing the e-0040 copula machinery.
formal_results: Type-I error at the null (delta_small=0) is well-calibrated across all cells (0.042-0.057, target 0.05), validating the z-test. Interaction (DiD) MDE = smallest small-model rough/fine gap (against a ZERO large-model gap) at 80% power, swept over small-model fine rate s_f in {0.05,0.10,0.20}: n=50 -> +0.25/+0.28/+0.31; n=63 -> +0.22/+0.24/+0.28; n=116 (within-deep supply-ample route) -> +0.16/+0.18/+0.20; n=200 -> +0.12/+0.13/+0.15. Versus e-0040's single-comparison MDE at matched n (n=50 +0.19-0.25; n=63 +0.16-0.21; n=116 +0.11-0.15; n=200 +0.08-0.11), the interaction surcharge is +0.04 to +0.07 absolute, i.e. the DiD MDE is ~1.3-1.4x the single-gap MDE.
results_description: Quantifies the e-0040 surcharge: detecting the capstone's gap-GROWS-as-model-shrinks INTERACTION costs ~1.3-1.4x the MDE of a single rough-vs-fine comparison. At the most affordable supply-ample size n=116 the eval can only flag the interaction if the small-model gap is >= +0.16-0.20 absolute against a zero large-model gap -- a huge granularity effect. And this UNDERSTATES the requirement: a real positive large-model gap (delta_large>0) shrinks the DiD to be resolved, needing yet more power. Type-I calibration (0.042-0.057) confirms the DiD test is valid, not the source of the pessimism.
conclusions: The capstone's interaction framing is decisively UNDERPOWERED at affordable eval sizes: the DiD MDE (+0.12 at n=200 up to +0.31 at n=50) sits far above any plausible few-point granularity effect, and is ~1.3-1.4x worse than e-0040's already-sobering single-gap MDE. Decision: the headline 'gap grows as the model shrinks' cannot be resolved by a single-seed paired pass@1 eval at any n the project can buy; the Modal go/no-go must drop the DiD significance framing in favor of (a) effect sizes with CIs across sizes, (b) pass@k>1 / continuous-margin outcomes, and (c) n well past 200, or restrict the paper's claim to a single-size gap (e-0040) rather than the interaction. CAVEAT: design-stage power calc; assumes exchangeable rho=0.5 across all four arms (same-base correlations likely exceed cross-size ones, which would RAISE power -- conservative there) and a clean zero large-model gap (optimistic); projects power, not a measured pass@k outcome.
